exercises.title: What happens without a timeout on an external call?
exercises.instructions: An external API hangs and never responds. Thread pool size = 200. Each call waits indefinitely. After 200 concurrent users hit this path, what happens?
exercises.expected_solution:
200 concurrent calls × indefinite wait = 200 threads all blocked.
Thread pool (200) is exhausted.
New requests queue up → eventually timeout waiting for a thread → HTTP 503/504.
The entire service is effectively DOWN because all threads are blocked on one slow dependency.

Fix: add timeout at HTTP client level (read timeout) + @TimeLimiter at application level.
With 2-second timeout: threads blocked for max 2 seconds each → freed → pool never exhausts.

test_cases.expected:
200 concurrent calls × indefinite wait = 200 threads all blocked.
Thread pool (200) is exhausted.
New requests queue up → eventually timeout waiting for a thread → HTTP 503/504.
The entire service is effectively DOWN because all threads are blocked on one slow dependency.

Fix: add timeout at HTTP client level (read timeout) + @TimeLimiter at application level.
With 2-second timeout: threads blocked for max 2 seconds each → freed → pool never exhausts.

hints: Thread pool exhaustion: no threads → all new requests fail
Timeout: upper bound on how long any thread can be blocked
Two timeout layers: HTTP client (network) + @TimeLimiter (application)

exercises.title: Complete @TimeLimiter application.yml
exercises.expected_solution:
resilience4j:
  timelimiter:
    instances:
      recommendations:
        timeoutDuration: 2s
        cancelRunningFuture: true
hints: timelimiter: the resilience4j section name
cancelRunningFuture: interrupts the async task on timeout

exercises.title: Add @TimeLimiter to a service
exercises.instructions: Write FlipkartRecommendationService.getRecommendations(): @TimeLimiter('recs', 2s) + @CircuitBreaker. Returns CompletableFuture. Fallback: cached bestsellers.
hints: @TimeLimiter REQUIRES CompletableFuture return type
Fallback also returns CompletableFuture<List<String>>
@TimeLimiter outer, @CircuitBreaker inner: timeout counts as CB failure

exercises.title: Compare 5 fallback strategies
exercises.instructions:
For 5 different Swiggy features, write the appropriate fallback strategy:
1. Delivery estimate: static default (45 min)
2. Restaurant list: cached data from Redis
3. Interactive seat map: degraded zone pricing
4. Payment processing: fail fast (throw exception)
5. Order confirmation SMS: queue for retry
hints: Static: return hard-coded safe value — simplest and most reliable
Fail fast: throw an exception — appropriate when silent degradation is worse than a clear error
Queue: deferred processing — user gets confirmation, delivery happens later

exercises.title: RestTemplate with connection and read timeout
exercises.instructions: Configure RestTemplate for Razorpay's bank verification API: connection timeout=2s, read timeout=5s. Also add @TimeLimiter(3s) at the application level. Explain why both are needed.
hints: setConnectTimeout: max time to establish TCP connection
setReadTimeout: max time to receive data after connection established
@TimeLimiter: application-level budget, works with CB

exercises.title: Test @TimeLimiter fires on slow call
exercises.instructions: Write a @SpringBootTest: mock engine that sleeps 3 seconds. @TimeLimiter set to 1 second. Verify: fallback is called and returns quickly (< 2 seconds total).
hints: Thread.sleep in mock: simulates slow service
elapsed < 2500: verify it didn't wait the full 3 seconds
future.get(): blocks caller until future completes (with timeout, this is fast)

exercises.title: Zerodha market data with timeout + cached fallback
exercises.instructions: ZerodhaMarketDataService.getLTP(symbol): @TimeLimiter(1s) + @CircuitBreaker. Fallback: return last known price from a ConcurrentHashMap cache. Cache updated on each successful call.
hints: Update cache on each success: priceCache.put() inside supplyAsync lambda
Fallback returns CompletableFuture<Double>: matches original return type
Stale price is acceptable for a few seconds; 0.0 signals 'price unknown'

exercises.title: Multi-level timeout configuration for different criticalities
exercises.instructions:
Write application.yml with three timeout profiles:
1. 'payments': 5s timeout (critical — users will wait)
2. 'recommendations': 1.5s (non-critical — fast fallback preferred)
3. 'reports': 30s (async batch — long but bounded)
Then write service methods for each using appropriate fallback strategies.
hints: Different timeouts for different SLAs: payment user will wait, recommendation user won't
cancelRunningFuture=false for long batch jobs: let the report continue even after client timeout
Payment fail-fast: better to tell user to retry than silently queue

exercises.title: BookMyShow: complete recommendation + payment with timeouts
exercises.instructions: Build BookMyShowRecommendationService: event recommendations (1.5s timeout, cached fallback) + ticket price lookup (3s timeout, default price fallback). @SpringBootTest: 2 tests verifying timeouts and fallbacks.
hints: try-catch reset: CB may not be registered until first use
CompletableFuture.get(): blocks test thread until future resolves
Two CBs: bms-recs and bms-price are independent circuits

exercises.title: Which fallback fires — timeout or circuit breaker?
hints: Outer annotation: timeout wraps the circuit breaker
OPEN circuit: CB fails fast without ever starting the timer
RuntimeException: CB catches it → CB fallback fires (inner wins over outer)

exercises.title: Razorpay: bank verification with timeout + async fallback
exercises.instructions: RazorpayBankVerificationService.verify(accountNumber): @TimeLimiter(3s) + @CircuitBreaker. On timeout: mark as PENDING_VERIFICATION (allow payment, verify async later). On success: return VERIFIED. Test: timeout triggers PENDING status.
hints: PENDING_VERIFICATION: allows payment to proceed without blocking on slow bank API
Thread.sleep(5000) > timeout(3s): should trigger timeout fallback
elapsed < 4000: verify it returned before the full 5 second sleep

exercises.title: All fallback strategies in one service
exercises.instructions: Build ZerodhaResilienceService with 4 different methods showcasing all 5 fallback strategies. @SpringBootTest (2 tests) verifying correct fallback behavior for 2 of the 5 strategies.
hints: Cache populated on first success; fallback reads from it
Queue for retry: message isn't lost, delivered when service recovers
Fail fast: throw exception — payment service failure should surface

exercises.title: Flipkart product page: full timeout + fallback design
exercises.instructions:
Build Flipkart product page service with 3 @TimeLimiter calls:
1. getProductDetails(id): 2s timeout, cached fallback
2. getReviews(id): 1s timeout, empty list fallback
3. getRelatedProducts(id): 1s timeout, bestsellers fallback
@SpringBootTest: 3 tests, all timeouts trigger correct fallbacks
hints: Three independent CBs: fk-product, fk-reviews, fk-related
Page still renders: product shows 'Loading', reviews empty, related shows bestsellers
All three fallbacks are safe — page always loads even if all three services fail

exercises.title: Async parallel calls with timeout using CompletableFuture.allOf
exercises.instructions: Build FlipkartProductLoader that calls 3 services in PARALLEL (not sequential) using CompletableFuture.allOf, each with 1-second individual timeout. Aggregate results with their fallbacks. Measure: parallel calls take ~1s total vs sequential 3s.
hints: Start all 3 futures before .get(): they run concurrently
allOf + get: waits for all to complete (with timeout)
Total time: max of individual times, not sum

exercises.title: Complete Flipkart product page resilience — M19 T4 synthesis
exercises.instructions: Full product page: 3 parallel @TimeLimiter calls + appropriate fallbacks + @CircuitBreakers + event listeners alerting on timeout. Test (3 tests): parallel load, individual timeout fallback, CB opens after timeouts.
exercises.starter_code: // Complete Flipkart product page resilience synthesis
hints: loadPage: starts all 3 CompletableFutures before any .get() — truly parallel
Page always loads: each section has a safe fallback
allOf timeout: 5s budget for ALL 3 calls combined (generous)
